subject: Chemistry
title: Mendeleev's Card Game
description: A 60-second lesson on Mendeleev's Card Game.

hook.text: Dmitri Mendeleev wrote each known element on a card and played a game of chemical solitaire.

buildup.text: By 1869, 63 elements were known. Several chemists tried to organize them, but nothing worked.

discovery.text: Mendeleev arranged cards by atomic weight and noticed properties repeated in regular intervals.

twist.text: Legend says the final arrangement came to him in a dream. He wrote it down the moment he woke.

climax.text: He published the periodic table in 1869. It organized all of chemistry in a single chart.

punchline.text: A card game solved chemistry. The table that explains matter.

quiz.question: How did Mendeleev organize the elements in his periodic table?
options: By atomic weight, noticing repeating properties
By the year they were discovered
